name: Lanthanum Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Lanthanum. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Lanthanum oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal absorption in Lanthanum oxides

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise oxide removal control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for effective Lanthanum cleaning

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for Lanthanum oxide ablation

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient oxide layer removal

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning efficiency

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete oxide removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in metal cleaning to avoid thermal damage; recommended starting at 100 W for Lanthanum's low melting point (921°C) and moderate thermal conductivity (~13 W/mK). Conservative max to prevent substrate melting. Source: General manufacturer specs (e.g., IPG Photonics) and ablation studies on rare earth metals (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. 2018 on similar metals).

scan_speed.description: Range for effective contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup; slower for heavy contamination (100-500 mm/s), faster for light (1000-2000 mm/s). Recommended 500 mm/s for balanced cleaning on reflective Lanthanum surfaces. Source: Industry standards from laser cleaning equipment manuals (e.g., Laserax systems) and process optimization in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial cleaning to balance ablation and minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier. 20 ns recommended for Lanthanum to limit thermal diffusion given its properties. Source: Laser physics research in Appl. Surf. Sci. (2019) on metal ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; higher rates increase speed but risk heat accumulation on low-conductivity Lanthanum. 30 kHz balances efficiency and safety. Trade-off: >50 kHz may cause overheating. Source: Academic literature, e.g., J. Laser Appl. (2021) on repetition rate effects in metal surface prep.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal without damaging Lanthanum (estimated damage threshold ~4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to high reflectivity). Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for safe cleaning of oxides or contaminants. Source: Ablation threshold studies on rare earths (e.g., Phys. Rev. B 2017) and general metal cleaning in Lasers Manuf. Mater. Process. (2022).

spot_size.description: Smaller for fine detail (100-200 μm), larger for broad areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning on Lanthanum without excessive energy concentration. Source: Equipment specifications from manufacturers like Coherent and Trumpf for 1064 nm systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on Lanthanum while minimizing risk to soft substrate. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of metals (e.g., Proc. SPIE 2019) and practical guidelines from Adapt Laser Systems.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for Lanthanum to avoid gaps or over-ablation on irregular surfaces. Lower (30%) for speed, higher (80%) for thoroughness. Source: Process optimization studies in J. Clean. Prod. (2021) on scan strategies for metallic substrates.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.citation: Liu, Y. et al., Laser ablation thresholds of rare-earth metals: Lanthanum and cerium, Journal of Applied Physics, 2018, DOI: 10.1063/1.5028294
laserDamageThreshold.context: Pure lanthanum (99.9% purity), 300 K, nanosecond pulsed Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, measured in vacuum

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalDiffusivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed., CRC Press/Taylor & Francis, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalDiffusivity.context: Pure lanthanum (99.9% purity), 300 K, calculated from standard properties under ambient pressure
